error_signature: HTTP request doesn’t give you a binary
title: S3 upload fails due to incorrect mime type
symptom: When attempting to upload an XML file to S3, the upload does not recognize the correct mime type, leading to potential issues with file handling.
applies_when: Using HTTP request to fetch files
not_this_if: Files are uploaded directly without HTTP requests
root_cause: The HTTP request node in n8n does not return binary data by default, which can result in the S3 upload not having the correct mime type for the file being uploaded. This can cause issues when the file is accessed later.
fix_steps: Add a code step between the HTTP request and the S3 upload node
Set the correct mime type in the code step
agent_instruction: Insert a code step to set the mime type before uploading to S3.
